mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% within the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts expand while industrial production remains flat or declines, it indicates a sectoral rotation toward consumer-facing services and away from cyclical manufacturing, boosting aggregate equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or periods of rising housing starts combined with falling industrial output consistently produce negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 240, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates real borrowing costs and suppresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the signal fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the denominator for corporate earnings estimates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity pricing models.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative or flat S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 60-day forward returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in total nonfarm payrolls below 142,000 signals weakening aggregate labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive forward returns in over 65% of instances where PAYEMS drops below the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and food services contract while the federal funds rate remains elevated, it indicates a demand shock amplified by tight financial conditions, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns, or periods of falling RSAFS with high FEDFUNDS consistently yield positive equity returns due to alternative liquidity channels.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 108, it reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and strong order backlogs, which translate into higher corporate capital expenditure and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High industrial production levels are followed by negative equity returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.
